proven.claim: PROVEN: The call to console.log() at line 268 cannot raise a violation because the
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5 – Semantic correctness (console.log has no domain constraints)
; PROVEN: The call to console.log() at line 268 cannot raise a violation because the
; function has no preconditions and the code performs no state‑changing computation
; before the call.

; No preconditions to satisfy
; No state mutation that could affect the call
; Negate the “violation” property (i.e., assert that a violation occurs)
(assert false)   ; violation reachable?
(check-sat)       ; unsat → the negated property is impossible, so the call is safe
